skill.name: writing-skills
skill.description: Use when creating, updating, organizing, or testing agent skills for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
skill.summary: Guides authors through skill structure, discovery metadata, templates, and testing practices.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but most occur in markdown examples, documentation links, or terminology and are false positives. The confirmed runtime risk is a bundled Node.js helper that reads files, writes generated diagram files, and invokes Graphviz with a hardcoded command. No evidence found of credential exfiltration, malicious network calls, persistence, or prompt-injection text that tries to override this audit.

medium_findings.title: Utility Script Executes Graphviz
medium_findings.description: The Node.js helper imports child_process and invokes the external Graphviz dot command. The command is hardcoded and receives DOT content through stdin, so command injection was not confirmed, but external tool execution requires user awareness.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly executes an external binary with execSync. The command string is fixed, which lowers injection risk but still creates runtime dependency and execution risk.

medium_findings.title: Utility Script Writes Generated Files
medium_findings.description: The diagram renderer accepts a skill directory, reads SKILL.md, creates a diagrams directory, and writes SVG and DOT output files. This is legitimate diagram-generation behavior, but it can modify the filesystem where the script is pointed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem writes are explicit and tied to documented output generation. No evidence found that it targets sensitive paths unless the user supplies such a directory.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Trigger Static Alerts
low_findings.description: Many external-command findings are markdown examples, shell snippets, or inline code used to teach skill creation. They are not automatically executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation snippets inside markdown files. They show examples for users and are not executable code paths.

low_findings.title: Documentation Links Flagged as Network Activity
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs in the documentation point to Anthropic documentation assets and images. No runtime network request code was found in the inspected files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are embedded in markdown documentation. They do not constitute automatic data exfiltration or active network code.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and C2 Keyword Alerts Are Semantic False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity keyword alerts appear to match ordinary documentation words, examples, and names inside writing guidance. No cryptographic implementation, beaconing behavior, or command-and-control logic was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Inspected context shows skill-authoring prose, not crypto code or malware infrastructure. The finding remains low because it comes from broad keyword matching.

low_findings.title: Home Directory Paths Are Instructional Examples
low_findings.description: Hidden home-directory paths are used to explain where users may install OpenCode skills. The markdown examples do not read or write those paths automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths appear in fenced documentation examples for skill placement. They are not connected to runtime code in the skill body.

dangerous_patterns.title: child_process.execSync in Helper Script
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper script uses execSync to check for Graphviz and render DOT diagrams. The external command is hardcoded, but invoking local binaries is a sensitive pattern.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous API is present and reachable when the helper script is run. Static evidence is strong, but semantic review shows a legitimate diagram-rendering purpose.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Writes in Helper Script
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper script creates a diagrams directory and writes SVG or DOT files below the supplied skill directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are explicit and expected for output generation. The risk depends on where the user chooses to run the script.

content.value_statement: Agent skills often fail because their triggers, structure, or tests are unclear. This skill provides templates, standards, and review guidance for building reliable skills for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Selects simple, expanded, or platform skill structures based on scope.
Provides templates for technique, reference, discipline, and pattern skills.
Explains discovery metadata, trigger phrases, naming rules, and directory layout.
Documents anti-rationalization methods for skills that enforce strict workflows.
Includes guidance for testing skills with realistic agent behavior scenarios.
Offers an optional Graphviz rendering script for diagram blocks in skill files.
limitations: It does not validate marketplace policy compliance automatically.
It focuses on skill-writing guidance, not general application development.
The optional diagram renderer requires Graphviz to be installed locally.
Examples may need adaptation for different skill runtimes or repository layouts.

use_cases.title: Create a New Skill
use_cases.description: Use the decision tree and templates to draft a focused skill with clear triggers and structure.

use_cases.title: Improve Skill Discovery
use_cases.description: Rewrite descriptions, tags, and metadata so agents load the right skill at the right time.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Skills
use_cases.description: Apply shared naming, testing, and documentation rules across a team skill library.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Skill Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose the right structure for a new agent skill. The skill will cover [scope], use [tools], and needs [reference materials].

prompt_templates.title: Draft Skill Metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Create metadata for a skill about [topic]. Include a Use when description, trigger keywords, category, and a concise skill name.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Skill Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill draft for clarity, trigger quality, file structure, and agent compliance risks. Suggest concrete edits.

prompt_templates.title: Design Skill Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Create realistic tests for this agent skill. Include success cases, failure cases, rationalization pressure, and expected agent behavior.

output_examples.input: I need a skill for debugging flaky async tests.
output_examples.output: Recommended structure: a Tier 1 technique skill. Use a trigger-focused description, include symptoms such as timeout and race condition, and add before-and-after examples.

output_examples.input: My skill is not being loaded when users ask relevant questions.
output_examples.output: Likely issue: discovery metadata is too broad or workflow-focused. Rewrite the description to start with Use when and include exact user symptoms.

output_examples.input: I want a strict TDD skill that agents cannot bypass.
output_examples.output: Use a discipline skill. Add explicit no-exception rules, a rationalization table, red flags, and tests under time pressure.

best_practices: Start descriptions with clear trigger conditions instead of workflow summaries.
Move detailed references out of SKILL.md and load them only when needed.
Test skills with realistic prompts that include ambiguity and deadline pressure.
anti_patterns: Writing long monolithic skills that consume context before details are needed.
Using generic names or descriptions that do not match real user symptoms.
Adding strict rules without testing how agents rationalize around them.

faq.question: What does this skill help me create?
faq.answer: It helps create and improve agent skills with clear triggers, structure, templates, and tests.

faq.question: Does it support Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is general enough for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code skill workflows.

faq.question: Does it write the whole skill for me?
faq.answer: It provides templates and review criteria. You still need to supply the domain knowledge and examples.

faq.question: When should I use the Tier 2 or Tier 3 references?
faq.answer: Use them when one file is not enough, or when the skill needs multiple related reference areas.

faq.question: Why does the skill discuss anti-rationalization?
faq.answer: Some skills enforce strict behavior. Anti-rationalization guidance helps close loopholes before agents ignore the rule.

faq.question: Does the included script run automatically?
faq.answer: No. The Graphviz helper runs only when a user explicitly executes the script.
